world.title: Australian travellers fingerprinted and face
world.description: Australians travelling to Europe will be fingerprinted and face-scanned at the border from today, as authorities activate a sweeping new digital system designed to track who enters and leaves the continent.

world.title: As DRC government spokesperson pledges to “strengthen protection for journalists,” RSF calls for concrete delivery
world.description: In a meeting with Reporters Without Borders (RSF), the Minister of Communication and Media and Spokesperson for the Congolese Government, Patrick Muyaya, committed to introducing  a series of press freedom measures, including alert systems for journalists in danger, reduced accreditation fees for foreign news professionals and training sessions for law enforcement agents on how to respect the press freedom law. The discussion followed the publication of the new RSF report “What It’s Like to Be a Journalist in Africa’s Great Lakes Region.” RSF welcomes this political will and urges the authorities to ensure the commitments are swiftly implemented with a clear plan of action.

world.title: Inside the church of Pete Hegseth, the man who has been leading the US through war

business.title: UBS Won’t Release Nazi Accounts Settlement Files Sought by Investigator After Court Setback
business.description: (Bloomberg) -- UBS Group AG says it won’t hand over a stash of privileged documents for a probe into Credit Suisse’s handling of Nazi-linked accounts after failing to win assurances that doing so wouldn’t expose it to new financial claims.

business.title: Kalshi now controls 89% of the U.S. prediction market as regulated trading takes over
business.description: As Kalshi captures a dominant 89% of the market, a high-stakes legal battle between federal regulators and individual states will determine if these platforms are treated as sophisticated financial tools or just another form of gambling.

science.title: Finding the Future of Eye Care with Frogs
science.description: Some of the most “ribbiting” research happening on campus stems from regeneration – the science of reforming, repairing, or regrowing lost tissue. This is the prerogative of researchers in the Tseng Lab, which recently observed that certain frog larvae can regrow their eyeballs. “We’re building a blueprint for eye tissue regeneration,” said Kelly Tseng, professor in the School of Life Sciences.

ai.description: MADISON, Wis. (AP) — Leaders on the board that oversees the Universities of Wisconsin rebuffed the fired system president's claim that he was “blindsided” by their decision to oust him, telling lawmakers Thursday that he was slow to address pressing issues like artificial intelligence and feared upsetting policymakers, faculty and staff.
